seo_title: How Retrieval-Augmented Spatiotemporal Modeling Improves Urban…
meta_description: Bridge, a retrieval-augmented spatiotemporal graph framework, outperforms competitive baselines in urban delivery demand forecasting with cold-start regions.
body_html:
Bridge improves urban delivery demand forecasting by leveraging a retrieval-augmented spatiotemporal graph framework.

Improves forecast accuracy in cold-start regions Enhances operational memory for future demand patterns Outperforms competitive baselines in urban delivery demand forecasting

Technical Approach Combines an inductive contextual graph backbone with a time-aware memory of region-time windows.

Experiments and Results DatasetPerformance MetricFour real-world delivery datasetsConsistently improves over competitive spatiotemporal baselines

Conclusion Retrieval augmentation provides a useful operational memory for cold-start urban demand forecasting when parametric graph generalization alone is insufficient.

source.title: Bridge: Retrieval-Augmented Spatiotemporal Modeling for Urban Delivery Demand
source.summary: Researchers have developed a new framework called Bridge, which improves the accuracy of urban delivery demand forecasting in newly added service regions without historical records. Unlike existing spatiotemporal forecasters, Bridge uses a retrieval-augmented approach to combine regional context and recent dynamics to predict future demand patterns. The framework has been tested on four real-world datasets and shown to outperform competitive baselines in both within-city cold-start scenarios and cross-city transfer with partial observations.
